description: Oracle test facts for Q_f ratio R_f = Q_{e^(-r)} / Q_{1/r} as stretch-resistant invariant.

facts.truth: a ratio of two Q_f with different kernels that cancels stretching effects

facts.context: Under pure stretching by factor s, the Q_f ratio R_f = Q_exp / Q_inv has fractional variance:
During Biot-Savart evolution of vortex rings, the Q_f ratio R_f:

facts.truth: ~0.006, which is 5x better than any single Q_f

distractors: the ratio is independent of geometry
exponential and inverse kernels are identical

facts.context: The physical interpretation of R_f = Q_{e^(-r)} / Q_{1/r} is:
facts.truth: balance between short-range (exponential) and all-scale (inverse) vortex interactions
